response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and web engineer with strong software craftsmanship fundamentals, but his profile does not align with the core requirements of this role. His C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over 25 years ago and a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not appear in his current skills list at all. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), MQTT, or industrial protocols such as Modbus or OPC UA. His career has been focused almost entirely on web, mobile, and cloud-native development rather than the high-performance, secure, on-premises systems engineering this role demands.
strengths: Extensive software architecture experience with event-driven and distributed systems, demonstrated across multiple roles and open-source projects
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record, including workshops, dojos, and public talks, which aligns with the team mentorship responsibility
Hands-on experience building high-throughput, low-latency systems (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second), showing performance-oriented thinking
concerns: C++ is absent from the candidate's current skill set; the only relevant C++ work is from 2000–2001 and a university thesis, making the core technical requirement essentially unmet
No demonstrated knowledge of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or industrial cybersecurity concepts required for this role
No experience with industrial automation platforms, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0), or MQTT-based IoT systems

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or cybersecurity domain experience
Strong distributed systems and architecture background
